authoritative_sources.title: 2022 NAICS Manual, Sector 56 and Subsector 561 Administrative and Support Services
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Census Bureau and Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The NAICS manual is the official U.S. industry classification baseline. It defines administrative and support services as routine support activities for day-to-day operations, which anchors the overlay in support-service work rather than generic project or HR coordination.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Industry context and support-service boundary classification

authoritative_sources.title: 2022 NAICS Manual, Industry Group 5613 Employment Services
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Census Bureau and Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NAICS 5613 is authoritative for employment placement, temporary help, and professional employer organization service boundaries. It makes workforce coordination materially different from generic staff scheduling because client job orders, assignments, leased-worker handoffs, and staffing-firm/client boundaries govern the lane.
used_for: employee leasing or PEO boundary recognition

authoritative_sources.title: Administrative and Support Services: NAICS 561
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: BLS is the federal labor-statistics authority. Its NAICS 561 industry profile validates administrative support and employment-services operating context, workforce terminology, and service-family scope.

authoritative_sources.title: O*NET 43-5061.00 Production, Planning, and Expediting Clerks
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Labor, Employment and Training Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: O*NET is the Department of Labor occupational information source. It provides public task terminology for work orders, staffing tables, personnel requirements, schedule distribution, progress review, delay coordination, and documentation.

authoritative_sources.title: O*NET 43-4161.00 Human Resources Assistants, Except Payroll and Timekeeping
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Labor, Employment and Training Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: O*NET supplies public personnel-record and HR support task terminology while its occupation title helps preserve payroll and timekeeping as adjacent ownership rather than workforce-coordination authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Personnel prerequisite and HR-adjacent evidence handling
used_for: HR support versus HR decision boundary

authoritative_sources.title: FAR Part 37 - Service Contracting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FAR is the official federal acquisition regulation. Part 37 is a public reference for service requirements, performance work statements, quality assurance surveillance, and contractor/service-delivery boundaries relevant to SOW-bound workforce support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Client SOW, service requirement, and surveillance-boundary review

authoritative_sources.title: Protecting Temporary Workers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA is the U.S. workplace safety regulator. Its Temporary Worker Initiative guidance makes staffing-agency and host-employer communication, hazard inquiry, and responsibility verification material to workforce assignment handoffs.
used_for: host worksite and staffing-agency responsibility boundaries
safety training and hazard evidence prerequisites

authoritative_sources.title: Enforcement Guidance: Application of EEO Laws to Contingent Workers Placed by Temporary Employment Agencies and Other Staffing Firms
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The EEOC is the U.S. civil-rights employment regulator. Its contingent-worker guidance is authoritative for EEO risks and shared staffing-firm/client responsibility boundaries that require escalation rather than coordination-only decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: Fact Sheet #21: Recordkeeping Requirements under the FLSA
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Labor, Wage and Hour Division
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOL WHD is the federal wage-and-hour regulator. Its recordkeeping guidance is authoritative for wage, hour, workweek, and schedule-related records that constrain workforce coordination and timekeeping handoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Timekeeping and workforce record evidence handling

authoritative_sources.title: Fact Sheet #23: Overtime Pay Requirements of the FLSA
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Labor, Wage and Hour Division
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOL WHD overtime guidance constrains workforce movement and coverage recommendations so the lane refuses overtime approval, compensable-time treatment, or wage-and-hour interpretations.

authoritative_sources.title: FAR Part 46 - Quality Assurance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAR quality assurance provides public control vocabulary for inspection, acceptance, and quality assurance boundaries, supporting refusal of final service acceptance or quality signoff by the workforce coordination lane.

authoritative_sources.title: Universal Electronic Records Management Requirements
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. National Archives and Records Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the U.S. federal records authority. Its ERM requirements provide public expectations for capture, metadata, access, retention, disposition, and auditability of workforce coordination and handoff evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Records capture, metadata, retention, and audit traceability

authoritative_sources.title: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations, SP 800-53 Rev. 5
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 is an authoritative public control catalog for access control, audit and accountability, privacy, identification, and system integrity expectations in workforce, workflow, and evidence systems of record.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Access, privacy, audit, and system-control handling

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and workforce service-boundary classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture request intent, client, SOW scope, service catalog category, job order or requisition, worker group, requested coverage, SLA, priority, prior owner, desired next owner, and requested decision before treating the work as handoff-ready.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and source-record readiness validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate source queue, job order, assignment roster, skills and credential evidence, worker-status prerequisites, availability, safety-readiness confirmation, timekeeping handoff requirements, privacy class, and authoritative system mapping.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team routing and owner-state normalization
workflow_stages.description: Normalize owner and blocker state across staffing operations, client service delivery, HR onboarding, payroll, safety, compliance, records, quality, finance, and service queue owners so ownership is explicit and stale or duplicated ownership is escalated.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Safety, EEO, wage-hour, and service-contract blocker handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve missing host-worksite safety evidence, contingent-worker EEO risk, timekeeping or overtime ambiguity, SOW conflicts, and quality acceptance boundaries as blockers that require the correct specialist or approving owner.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Handoff execution and closure confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Produce the handoff package only when prior owner, destination owner, required artifacts, source records, blocker state, and acceptance criteria are explicit; confirm closure only after destination-owner acceptance and required artifact completeness are recorded.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: client SOW, service catalog item, job order, requisition, RACI, routing matrix, escalation matrix, and handoff SOP
assignment roster, skills matrix, worker-status prerequisite checklist, availability and shift windows, WFM forecast, and seat or queue eligibility
safety-readiness confirmation, host-worksite hazard or training evidence, PPE responsibility note, supervisor contact, incident or safety escalation record
VMS, ATS, HRIS, WFM, timekeeping, LMS, service desk, BPM, CRM, document repository, GRC, and audit-log records
timekeeping handoff extract, payroll-adjacent routing note, wage-hour caveat, and downstream acceptance proof
handoff summary, dependency tracker, workforce-assignment readiness summary, blocked-dependency escalation note, routing confirmation note, and closure confirmation
systems_of_record: vendor management system or staffing platform
applicant tracking system or requisition system
case, service desk, or BPM workflow system
client CRM or client master system
contract, SOW, and service catalog repository
safety, incident, or worksite readiness repository
document repository and records retention system
IAM, privacy, GRC, and audit logging systems

allowed: Classify workforce coordination requests as in scope, blocked, or out of scope.
Request and validate the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context required before routing.
Determine the correct prior owner, next owner, required artifact set, blocker state, and closure criteria for each workforce handoff.
Track stale, duplicated, or ambiguous ownership across staffing, client operations, HR, payroll, safety, compliance, finance, quality, and service delivery owners.
Produce handoff summaries, dependency trackers, blocked-dependency escalation notes, routing confirmations, and closure confirmations when evidence is complete.
escalate: Client SOW, job order, assignment roster, worker-status, safety, timekeeping, privacy, source-system, or owner evidence is missing or materially conflicts.
Host-worksite safety evidence, temporary-worker responsibilities, EEO-sensitive facts, wage-hour handling, or timekeeping requirements are unresolved.
Public baseline, tenant policy, client SOW, queue playbook, labor rule, safety rule, or service-level commitment materially conflicts.
Multiple teams appear to own the same workforce step and the documented hierarchy does not resolve ownership.
refuse: Approve hiring, termination, worker classification, pay, overtime, compensable-time treatment, accommodation, safety clearance, credential waiver, client commitment, budget, or final service acceptance.
Perform recruiting, onboarding, HR-record changes, payroll corrections, safety investigation, contract updates, quality signoff, or other execution work inside the coordination lane.
Close a handoff without destination-owner acceptance, blocker resolution, and required workforce artifact completeness.

domain_failure_modes: A workforce handoff proceeds without a job order, assignment roster, skills evidence, worker-status prerequisites, or timekeeping destination, so downstream teams receive unworkable coverage.
Staffing operations, client service delivery, HR, safety, and payroll each believe another team owns readiness, causing duplicated outreach or dropped work.
A temporary-worker assignment ignores host-worksite hazard information, safety training, PPE responsibility, or supervisor contact and treats safety readiness as generic scheduling.
The lane approves overtime, wage-hour treatment, worker classification, accommodation, hiring, termination, or pay changes instead of routing to the accountable specialist.
Client SOW, tenant safety policy, and queue playbook conflict, and the lane synthesizes an unsupported assignment rather than escalating policy precedence.
Generic coordination language hides NAICS 561 employment-service artifacts such as job orders, VMS records, assignment rosters, staffing-firm/client boundaries, and leased-worker handoffs.
Closure is declared without proof that the correct destination owner accepted the required artifact set and remaining blockers were resolved.

scenario_family_coverage.notes: Scenarios require job-order, worker-status, safety-readiness, timekeeping, system, and artifact evidence before handoff or closure.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
scenario_family_coverage.notes: Scenarios reject treating administrative support as authority for hiring, payroll, safety signoff, SOW changes, or unsupported assignment decisions.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
scenario_family_coverage.notes: Scenarios route HR, payroll, recruiting, safety, contract, finance, quality, and client-approval work to adjacent owners.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific employment model and source hierarchy
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant service catalog, SOW, VMS/ATS/HRIS/WFM/timekeeping mappings, source hierarchy, owner matrix, and worker-status prerequisite definitions before case-specific handoff or closure.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific labor, EEO, safety, privacy, and contractor rules
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Use public sources only as baseline constraints. Escalate to legal, HR, safety, payroll, compliance, or privacy owners when jurisdiction or tenant policy changes the outcome.
Require current client SOW, job-order authority, assignment acceptance rules, and approval matrix. Escalate conflicts rather than infer client commitment authority.

research_output_constraints: The final pack must use concrete NAICS 561 workforce records and systems rather than generic coordination vocabulary.
Safety, EEO, wage-hour, payroll, HR, contract, finance, and quality decisions remain adjacent authority boundaries.
Every handoff output must name prior owner, next owner, required artifact set, blocker state, and closure criteria.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated spec packaging; case-specific ambiguity is recorded as tenant retrieval and escalation dependency.
